TextENGNew: Hee hee... how interesting, what I've found.
To think something like this would turn up in this world...
You see... this may look like a Fragment, but it isn't one at all.
It bears a striking resemblance -- the same shape, the same color -- but it is something else entirely.
If I had to give it a name... let's call it 'Ishi' for now, shall we?
changeReason: Consistent naming change from 'concrete' to 'Ishi' preserving JPN wordplay on イシ (stone/will/intent/dying wish).

TextENGNew: Left alone, it would be nothing more than a mere 'stone'... but this 'Ishi' possesses a somewhat different property from a Fragment.
changeReason: Consistent naming change from 'concrete' to 'Ishi' preserving JPN wordplay on イシ (stone/will/intent/dying wish).

TextENGNew: Until now, the Fragments you've gathered have been crystallizations of will -- studded with the many fates surrounding Rika Furude and the intentions of countless people.
But... this has nothing to do with Rika Furude's future, much less her past. In the world of Fragments, it is a meaningless, worthless thing.
And yet... oh my?
How curious. Hee hee...
By touching this 'Ishi,' the Fragments scattered here and there have changed their very forms.
changeReason: Consistent naming change from 'concrete' to 'Ishi' preserving JPN wordplay on イシ (stone/will/intent/dying wish).

TextENGNew: Angular shapes have become rounded.
Distorted forms have become orderly.
Curves became straight lines, stagnant colors turned vivid, and what had been too murky to see through became transparent and perfectly clear...
...Did you notice?
Originally, even when all the Fragments were assembled, gaps remained and they could never form a complete shape.
Because the Fragment shards that should have filled those gaps had scattered and gone missing.
If they could be found... yes, perhaps a different future might open up.
If you can reach that place... or if you've already experienced it... I would love it if you could tell me someday what kind of world it was.
...Ah, that's right.
We were talking about the 'Ishi.'
changeReason: Consistent naming change from 'concrete' to 'Ishi' preserving JPN wordplay on イシ (stone/will/intent/dying wish).

TextENGNew: By coming into contact with this 'Ishi,' each Fragment altered its shape, its color, even its very substance... and they merged into one great mass.
changeReason: Consistent naming change from 'concrete' to 'Ishi' preserving JPN wordplay on イシ (stone/will/intent/dying wish).

TextENGNew: If I were to put it into words... perhaps I should call it the place where 'dreams' reside?
changeReason: Original simply calls it a 'dream'; new renders it as 'the place where dreams reside,' better capturing the JPN 夢のありか (the whereabouts/dwelling of dreams).

TextENGNew: That is to say, this mass contains... what everyone living within it wished for, somewhere deep in their hearts.
Their feelings, their longings... a crystallization of the future everyone wished for -- for their own happiness, and for the sake of the precious people each of them prayed to protect.
...Not everything turned out the way they had hoped.
It contained much self-reproach, much reflection... and a great deal of regret, the wish to do things over if only they could.
Those who were left behind remembered.
...They could not forget.
And that is precisely why... this 'Ishi' will now manifest its true purpose, here and now.
changeReason: Consistent naming change from 'concrete' to 'Ishi' preserving JPN wordplay on イシ (stone/will/intent/dying wish).

TextENGNew: It governs the 'intent' of thinking of someone.
changeReason: Naming change from 'concrete intent' to 'intent' (意思) preserving JPN homophone wordplay.

TextENGNew: It unifies the 'will' that someone desires.
changeReason: Naming change from 'concrete will' to 'will' (意志) preserving JPN homophone wordplay.

TextENGNew: It is guided by the 'dying wish' that someone has entrusted.
changeReason: Naming change from 'concrete final wishes' to 'dying wish' (遺志) preserving JPN homophone chain.

TextENGNew: This place is no longer June of Showa 58.
changeReason: New preserves the Japanese era name 'Showa 58' instead of converting to 1983, which is significant for the narrative transition to Heisei era mentioned in the next line.

TextENGNew: The era has changed to Heisei... children have grown into adults, adults have aged into the elderly -- a world of possibilities, in a sense.

``...The above constitutes the National Police Agency's guidelines for media response in this case.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2002. Are there any comments?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2003. If there are none, please relay this to the Metropolitan Police's First Investigation Division and adopt it as our response policy going forward...``
``W-Wait a moment!``

With a sharp clatter, Chief Inspector Junji Sakaguchi -- who had only just been appointed as the press liaison chief days before -- shot to his feet with such force he nearly knocked his chair over, and leaned forward toward the executive seats.
Watching this unfold with a sidelong glance, the chairperson of the meeting -- Senior Superintendent Tomoe Minai, Director of the Public Relations Office -- allowed herself a quiet, knowing smile.
(Just as I thought. It's him.)
She had been certain he would challenge this decision.
He had been known since his previous post as a detective for his uncommon hot-blooded dedication to fulfilling his duties with integrity -- a rarity in this day and age.

Of course... that very trait had earned him the disfavor of superiors who preferred to handle things quietly, and he had been sidelined until he was on the verge of being transferred on what amounted to trumped-up pretexts.
``Chief Sakaguchi... do you have an objection?``
``Director, I realize that as a newcomer speaking without a full grasp of the situation, I am being presumptuous... but I cannot accept this!``
``Why are we limiting the information released to the press to the bare facts of this Iwatani suspect's killing?``
``Shouldn't we also be disclosing the victim's background, the evidence of her drug use... and the movements of those pulling the strings behind the scenes?!``
``'Behind the scenes'... which part of the background are you referring to?``
``All of it, obviously!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2006. Someone who has accumulated the wealth and status that Yumi Iwatani has wouldn't commit murder over a simple lovers' quarrel!``
``Even the viewers consuming media coverage should have already grasped that much!``
``In fact, the internet is already buzzing with speculation about whether Yumi Iwatani was using drugs!``
``What's more, they've apparently even dug up the identity of the apartment owner at the crime scene!``

``My... internet users certainly are talented at gathering information.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2008. If only they'd stop shutting themselves indoors and put those skills to use in the media industry, or start their own news agencies.``
``Please don't make light of this!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2011. ...And besides!``

``Not just the First Investigation Division -- the Fifth Organized Crime Division has corroborated the fact that Yumi Iwatani was using banned substances, and they have identified by name the patron who was providing both the drugs and the venue for the transactions!``
changeReason: New provides accurate full names of police divisions (First Investigation Division, Fifth Organized Crime Division) instead of abbreviated forms.

TextENGNew: ``Then shouldn't we be requesting the First Division to release all of this information to the press?!``
Sakaguchi said this while pointing out the omissions in the current media guidelines.
...What he said was certainly reasonable, and everyone present thought he had a point.
(In principle, at least...)
However, the other staff understood well that this decision reflected some deliberate calculation on Tomoe's part.
And so they watched the man who had so bravely risen to his feet with the same eyes one might use to regard one's own former self, fresh from assignment.

``...Chief Sakaguchi.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2011. This murder case is a matter of considerable concern regarding its social impact.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2012. That is precisely why the Metropolitan Police has come to us for a judgment call.``
...A strangled male corpse had been found in a room of a certain luxury apartment.
Subsequent police investigation led to the arrest of the victim's romantic partner, a celebrity named Yumi Iwatani, who had fled the scene.
Iwatani was a popular artist with fans across a wide age range, and her fame was considerable.
Consequently, the media had been in a frenzy day and night over this celebrity's act of violence and spectacular fall from grace.
changeReason: Original says 'loss of a celebrity'; JPN 有名人の凶行と失墜ぶり means 'a celebrity's violence and fall from grace,' which the new correctly captures.

TextENGNew: (Less a public spectacle... more like a sacrificial offering.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2014. They've secured content that drives ratings. Internally, they're probably cheering.)
changeReason: Original translates 生贄 as 'scapegoat'; it actually means 'sacrificial offering/sacrifice,' which the new correctly uses.

TextENGNew: Of course... the Public Relations Office hadn't been asked to handle media decisions simply because the suspect was famous.
The real problem was that the male victim had been in contact with multiple women besides Iwatani, and was suspected of being a dealer who supplied banned substances.

In fact, the Metropolitan Police's Fifth Organized Crime Division had launched an investigation to round up the entire network behind the illicit trade routes, and in the process had identified this man and been letting him operate for months to build their case.
changeReason: New provides more accurate translation of 背後関係と取引ルートを一斉検挙 as 'round up the entire network of back-channel dealings,' rather than original's vaguer phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...But in the meantime, Iwatani -- who had been carrying on an affair with the man -- got into an argument when he tried to break things off, and in a moment of desperation, killed him.
changeReason: Original incorrectly says 'he had an affair with Iwatani'; JPN says Iwatani was the one having the affair, and the man initiated the breakup, not a mutual event.

TextENGNew: ...He was killed in what appeared to be a crime of passion.
However, traces of drug use had been found on her person as well, and it was already understood that she had likely obtained the drugs through the murdered man.
In that case... it was only natural to suspect that the ringleaders had instigated Iwatani to silence him, so that the police could not dig any deeper into the drug trade routes.
changeReason: Original's meaning is nearly opposite: it discusses 'tightening suspicion around Iwatani would cut off police access'; new correctly translates the JPN about ringleaders instigating Iwatani to silence the victim to prevent police investigation.

TextENGNew: As evidence of this, the names of several illicit dealers believed to have made contact with her had surfaced in the investigation, and the Fifth Division was still actively monitoring their movements.
``Certainly... this should not be treated as a mere murder, but as a large-scale conspiracy to kill.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2016. However, we have no evidence of that at present.``
``If that's the case, then we should refrain from releasing extraneous information, precisely to suppress the spread of speculation and rumor.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2018. ...Don't you agree?``

``Yes, I do understand what you're saying, Director...@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2015. But!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2016. That would make the murdered man appear to be nothing more than a pitiful, blameless victim!``
``This man toyed with countless lives through the drug trade and made them taste unspeakable suffering!``
``We should expose those facts and bring to light the crimes of the people behind him!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2019. Isn't that the role of police public relations?!``
``That would be no different from a gossip rag... a trial by rumor.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2020. It's clearly outside our duties... no, it would constitute an abuse of authority.``
``And setting aside the risk of a defamation lawsuit -- we can handle that -- have you ever considered the harm that the victim's family and associates would suffer as a result?``

``But... even so!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2021. He committed crimes!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2022. And there are still people out there who've escaped justice!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2023. You're telling me to just look the other way...?``
Sakaguchi trailed off mid-sentence, catching his breath as if something had suddenly occurred to him.
And though he loathed himself for harboring such an ugly suspicion, he forced the words out, hurling the thought like a challenge.
``Could it be... that this is pressure from above?``

``The owner of that apartment is a tycoon famous in the IT sector, with connections not only in the business world but in political circles too...!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2026. Don't tell me there were negotiations from that direction...?!``
``Chief Sakaguchi... you're going too far.``
``B-But...!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2028. If that's not the case, then what explanation is there for this media restriction--``
``...Sakaguchi-kun.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2023. Why do you think they use celebrities in commercials for food, apparel, and cosmetics?``
Abruptly.
Blindsided by a topic that had absolutely nothing to do with the discussion at hand, Sakaguchi was momentarily at a loss for words.
Even so, seeing that the eyes of his superior held not a trace of humor, he reluctantly organized his thoughts and squeezed out an answer.

``That would be... self-projection, a compensatory behavior, wouldn't it?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2031. By purchasing the same product used by a famous celebrity -- someone admired for their talent or looks -- you obtain the same thing they have.``
``Through that, the buyer can engage in a kind of imaging... and feel as though they've acquired the same abilities or appearance.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2033. ...Is that what you're getting at?``

``Precisely.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2025. ...Now apply that same logic.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2026. If the media were to reveal that a celebrity as famous as Iwatani had been using a banned substance... what do you think would happen to that drug's public awareness?``

``If an entertainer of her stature were found to be regularly using that drug...@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2028. her fans, or anyone else with even passing interest, would naturally want to find out what it is.``
``And then they'll start wondering where to obtain it, what its effects are...@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2030. The contamination of the general public by drugs would only spread further.``
``...They anticipated all of this when they set Yumi Iwatani up as the killer -- she was their advertisement.``
changeReason: Old says 'spreading propaganda portraying Iwatani as a criminal' vs new says 'set Iwatani up as the killer — she was their advertisement'; JPN 仕立て上げた implies framing, but the propaganda/criminal characterization is partially captured in old.

TextENGNew: ``Of course, they've surely compensated her adequately for it.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2033. Apparently her family got buried in enormous debt from some financial speculation a few years back.``
changeReason: Original misreads 本人にちゃんと支払っている as 'the person should pay the price'; it actually means 'they have properly paid/compensated her,' which the new correctly translates.

TextENGNew: ``And on top of that, Iwatani had been exhausted both mentally and physically from her private affairs, and had been contemplating retiring from show business entirely...``
``So all that's left is to make one last score, endure a stint in prison, and retire abroad after release.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2036. ...An ideal second life, wouldn't you say?``
``Director Minai... you've deduced that much...?!``
changeReason: Old renders そこまで as 'would you go that far' (moral judgment) vs new 'you've deduced that much' (amazement at insight); the Japanese is ambiguous but the investigative context favors the deduction reading.

TextENGNew: Tomoe responded to the stunned Sakaguchi's stare with a light shrug, as if to say yes.
The Public Relations Office was not merely a liaison for the press.

It precisely investigated the origins and trajectory of information, analyzed and grasped the details, and issued directives to all relevant parties -- it was, in essence, the National Police Agency's intelligence division.
Of course, the true scope of these duties was disclosed only to a handful of senior figures, Tomoe among them.
Thus not only Sakaguchi, but all the junior staff as well stared wide-eyed with astonishment.
(Perhaps... I said a bit too much.)
She inwardly reflected on her carelessness in revealing classified information in the heat of the moment, but on the other hand, she reconsidered that this was perhaps a good opportunity.
The staff present here were elite operatives whom Tomoe had personally selected from various fields and trained since becoming Director.
Even the newcomer Sakaguchi had come highly recommended by the previous Director for his skill and conviction.
Therefore, in preparation for future operations that were expected to grow ever more complex and multifaceted, she had wanted to cultivate a certain mindset in them right here and now.

``Sakaguchi-kun.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2039. Your sense of justice is commendable -- and I mean that without flattery.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2040. So you need not bend it, and I have no desire to see you compromise.``

``However... we in the police don't catch criminals and expose their misdeeds just to rack up points.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2042. We think, we act, and we take appropriate measures so that crime itself will disappear from this society.``
``And to that end, sometimes a seemingly roundabout approach is necessary... wouldn't you agree?``

``Everyone here, remember this.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2045. The role of the Public Relations Office is not simply to assist in apprehending criminals.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2046. Preventing the secondary spread of crime is equally vital to our mission.``
``The criminals are already thinking several steps beyond the crime itself.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2048. ...So we have no choice but to stay even further ahead.``
``A single move by public relations can make information from the police either effective or meaningless.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2050. Each and every one of you, keep that importance firmly in mind.``
changeReason: Original says 'one aspect of PR is understanding whether information is effective or meaningful'; new correctly says 'a single move by PR can make police information either effective or meaningless.'

TextENGNew: ``...@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2039. You're absolutely right... I wasn't thinking deeply enough.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_sakagu2040. But even so... having to stand by and watch the ringleaders get away with their crimes is... frustrating.``
``...Who said anything about letting them get away?``
Her voice rang sharp and cold as a blade of ice. Not just Sakaguchi but every staff member in the room snapped their heads up at once... a tremor like an electric shock running down their spines.

Gone was the gentle demeanor of the fiftyish 'office matriarch' from moments before. In her place stood a woman who could only be described as a fierce heroine, fixing her gaze squarely on the assembled staff with a knowing grin.
changeReason: Original uses 'heroine' for 烈女; new uses 'a woman who could only be described as formidable,' better capturing the JPN term's meaning of a fierce/resolute woman.

TextENGNew: ``It's true that there has been political pressure.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2053. ...So we'll turn it to our advantage.``
changeReason: Original says 'we're taking a political approach'; JPN 政治的にアプローチがあった means 'there has been political pressure/approach from politicians,' which the new correctly conveys.

TextENGNew: ``Once those people learn the content of our media release, they'll jump to the conclusion that we're being accommodating and let their guard down.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2055. Then they'll prepare a new dealer and establish new trade routes.``
``...But that will be their endpoint.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2057. The Fifth Division is already laying the groundwork for their encirclement.``

``On top of that, I've quietly leaked information to the Narcotics Control Department at the Ministry of Health so they can serve as beaters to flush out the prey.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2059. ...Of course, the top brass at the National Police Agency probably haven't anticipated a scheme this deep.``
changeReason: Original garbles the meaning about the narcotics department; new correctly explains that Tomoe has secretly leaked info to the Narcotics Control Department to act as beaters in the operation.

TextENGNew: The upper echelons' negotiations with politicians, bureaucrats, and power brokers were no doubt driven by their own calculations.
...But that was none of Tomoe's organization's concern.
Even if it meant incurring the displeasure of certain powerful figures... the police had an unwavering standard and a firm role to fulfill.
That role was to pursue crime fairly and relentlessly... and to break the will of evildoers so thoroughly that they would abandon 'evil' itself.
As long as civilization endures and desire dwells in human hearts, crime and wrongdoing will never vanish.
But as long as the deterrent force never loses its raison d'etre, society should ultimately move in a better direction.
That was Tomoe's unwavering philosophy, unchanged since the day she first took her oath...!

``Sakaguchi... don't underestimate the Public Relations Office.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2061. We have no use for the naive notion that a few traded punches can settle everything.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2062. If you're going to act, think about how to crush them utterly.``
``That is precisely why I brought you here -- because you've witnessed more than enough absurdity in the field as a detective and tasted the bitter fruits of it.``
changeReason: Original says 'I called all of you here'; JPN says あなたをここに呼んだ (I called YOU here), specifically addressing Sakaguchi, not everyone.

TextENGNew: Tomoe's words, laced with iron authority, struck Sakaguchi like a bolt of lightning through his entire body, and before he knew it he had snapped into a salute.
...In that instant, every trace of the idea that he had been 'demoted' to the Public Relations Office vanished from his mind without a trace.
In its place, what welled up inside his chest was a sense of mission... and the detective's soul he had all but forgotten...

``...Yes, that's right.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2065. Just as you said, Maeno-san -- he was clearly radiating contempt at first.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2066. The classic reaction of a frustrated elite, I suppose.``
``Well, I gave him a good talking-to right off the bat... so I don't expect any trouble going forward.``

``Hahaha... say what you will, the man's still young.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2002. But he's earnest at heart and has good instincts, and my own people vouch for his potential.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2003. With the right training, I think he'll turn into quite the asset.``

``Hehe... I felt the same way.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2069. It takes real nerve to speak up with your own opinions like that on your very first day.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2070. I intend to keep an eye on him and develop him carefully over time.``
``...Though he still seems to have a strong desire to return to detective work.``
``...Reminds me of you when you first came to the Public Relations Office.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2005. So?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2006. Do you still want to go back to being a detective?``

``Ahahaha, come on... don't tease me!@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2073. I'm nearly old enough to be called 'grandma.'@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2074. If I ran around pulling all-nighters like I did when I was young, I'd keel over on the spot!``
``Ahahahaha!!``
On the other end of the cell phone, her superior Maeno was roaring with hearty laughter.
...Good grief. He'd risen high enough in the National Police Agency that you could count his rank from the top on one hand, yet his frank, unfiltered manner hadn't changed one bit.
Muttering that to herself, Tomoe pulled the phone slightly away from her mouth and let out a quiet chuckle.
``By the way... are you free tonight?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2009. I've got plans to go out for drinks.``

``You really never change, do you?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2076. Instead of sweet-talking an old woman like me, why not invite some younger lady?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2077. Shall I bring along some promising girls from PR?``

``Don't be ridiculous! I don't make a habit of flirting with married women!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2011. Actually, tonight I've been invited for drinks by none other than the chief of the Tokyo District Prosecutor's Special Investigation Department.@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2012. Sorimachi will be there too, so how about you?``

``...I can see right through your little scheme.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2079. Let me be clear -- I will absolutely not let Azusa become a police officer.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2080. And a prosecutor is out of the question too.``
``That girl's future is hers to decide as she pleases.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2082. Make sure you tell my husband that, loud and clear!``

``Wa ha ha ha!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2014. But you know, when his own boss, the Special Investigation chief, personally 'requests' something of Sorimachi...?@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2015. If he refuses, his next posting might be Abashiri or Ishigaki Island...``

``There are no prosecutor's offices in places like that!@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2084. Oh, fine, I understand!@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2085. I'll attend the dinner properly!@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2086. Happy now?!``
``OK, OK -- that's perfect!@k@vS20/00/VTK2_maeno2017. See you tonight, then!``
Maeno's satisfied voice was followed by a click as the call ended.
...Tomoe let out a sigh, flipped her phone shut, and slid it into her pocket.
``Honestly... so pushy.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2088. Just because Yuuichi-kun became a detective doesn't mean they need to start headhunting Azusa already.``
...To begin with, Tomoe's daughter Azusa was still in high school, in that sensitive period with college entrance exams looming the following year.
And yet they wanted her to choose between the bar exam and the national civil service exam at this stage? How could they expect that not to overwhelm the poor girl?

Well, when the right-hand subordinate of the Special Investigation Department's chief allowed his own son to become a police officer -- a detective, no less -- the father had apparently caught considerable flak from his superiors...
``That's perfectly fine, isn't it?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2090. Thanks to that, I now have an adorable successor -- I couldn't be happier.``
Murmuring this to herself, Tomoe stepped out from the National Police Agency's entrance into the bright, warm sunlight.
Her destination was the building next door: Room Seven of the Metropolitan Police Department's Public Security Bureau.
This was the workplace of Tomoe Minai's -- or properly, Tomoe Sorimachi's -- 'successor.'
``Oh, Minai-sa--@k@|@y@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2002. I mean, Mom.``
The young female detective, who had been reviewing documents, noticed Minai entering the room and immediately rose to her feet in a salute.
Tomoe laughed wryly at the stiff, overly earnest reaction and casually raised her right hand in response.
``Come on... I told you there's no need to force yourself to call me that when you're still not used to it.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2092. Just carry on as you always have.``

``Ah, ahahaha...@k@|@y@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2004. But Dad told me I should be proper about these things.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2005. He said it would be strange if I didn't call you Mom, or Tomoe-san at least...``
``That's so like Akasaka-san... hehe.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2094. But it's fine -- 'Minai-san' is perfectly all right.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2095. It feels more natural for us that way.``
Saying this, Tomoe gave the detective -- Miyuki Sorimachi -- a soft pat on the head.
Miyuki's face flushed with a mix of bashfulness and warmth as she looked up at Tomoe with a smile that was almost childlike.
``By the way, how did it go -- the situation in Hinamizawa?``

``Well... quite a lot happened.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2007. I stumbled onto a murder scene, got caught up in a shootout... and a ghost even showed up.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2008. It was hardly what you'd call a restful trip... it was absolutely full of thrills.``
``Full of thrills...?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2098. What on earth happened?``
``Well, you see...``
Then, for a while.
Miyuki gave Tomoe a condensed account of the events in Hinamizawa.
The many strange and extreme events that had transpired in just two days were extraordinarily fascinating... and by the time the account was finished, nearly an hour had passed.
``...Oh, sorry, Minai-san.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2010a. I got carried away and went on much too long...``
``Not at all -- it was fascinating, so I don't mind.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2100. Next time you visit, do tell me the rest in detail.``
``R-Right, absolutely!``
Miyuki's expression brightened instantly, and she nodded eagerly.

For Miyuki, Tomoe was a mentor from her police academy days -- the person who had sternly yet thoughtfully taught her the mindset of a police officer, and whom she respected more than anyone aside from her parents.
And when she began dating the man who would become her husband, Yuuichi Sorimachi, she had been both astonished and overjoyed to learn that marrying him would make Tomoe her mother-in-law.
(Though, since it was a remarriage, Tomoe had no blood relation to Yuuichi himself.)

Later, when she had wavered over whether to transfer from the Fourth Division to the Public Relations Office or the Public Security Bureau, she had trusted Tomoe's advice to prioritize her father's wishes and chosen Public Security.
Furthermore, Tomoe got along well with Miyuki's birth mother Yukie, and the Sorimachi and Akasaka families had built a warm, happy relationship as one extended family.
``...So?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2102. I received the exterior photos by email... but what was the situation inside?``

``Hmm, the readings were far too high... so we've decided to return with better equipment and dispatch personnel at a later date.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2013. ...However, your hunch was right on the mark, Minai-san.``
``It clearly was not volcanic toxic gas.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2015. If anything, it appears to be a type of nerve agent.``
Miyuki's cheerful demeanor vanished as she said this, and she carefully spread the photos she had taken on-site across the desk.
...What they depicted was an old shed, sitting quietly amid densely overgrown thickets.
It was a storage shed built on the former grounds of the Sonozaki main estate.
``...I confirmed it by asking people connected to the Sonozaki family. Apparently this is a hidden passage that leads through to the other side of the mountain.``
``It was quite deep, and from outside you'd never suspect its existence... If you fled into it, you could make your escape.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2018. Although...``

``The interior was saturated with deadly gas... anyone who entered would fall comatose within minutes and die.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2104. ...Whether they'd end up in heaven or hell.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2105. Either way, reaching the original exit would be impossible.``

``I believe the canisters of poison gas used at the time were most likely abandoned inside.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2020. ...By the way, Minai-san, how did you realize the toxic gas wasn't volcanic in origin?``

``For one thing, there's the topographical issue.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2107. Even if gas had erupted from the Onigafuchi Swamp, the original suspected source... considering the altitude gradient, it would take a considerable amount of time to reach the school that served as a refuge.``

``It would be a close thing whether it could even reach lethal concentration in a full twenty-four hours.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2109. If a strong wind from a low-pressure system had been blowing, that might change things, but the atmospheric pressure patterns at the time showed no sign of that... so it seems unlikely.``

``...Geologists have been saying that for quite some time.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2022. However, the counterargument is that the gas actually began seeping out several days before the disaster... and the residents simply didn't notice.``
``True enough.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2111. But there's another issue... the residual elements of volcanic gas.``

``The bulk of toxic volcanic gas consists of hydrogen sulfide, hydrogen chloride, and sulfurous acid gas... but based on what I've heard about the condition of the victims' remains, there's no hydrogen sulfide.``

``The skin of someone who has inhaled hydrogen sulfide discolors to a degree visible at a glance.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2114. Hydrogen chloride can be ruled out for similar reasons.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2115. And given the instantaneous lethality, sulfurous acid gas is also eliminated...``
``Then what about asphyxiation from carbon dioxide... or carbon monoxide?``

``There was apparently a survivor at the time.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2117. A boy who woke up by the riverbank and walked all the way to the branch school.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2118. ...The distance makes it impossible for anyone to have moved through a concentration severe enough to cause asphyxiation without breathing.``

``Based on all these hypotheses and the forensic examination of the remains... I concluded it was nerve gas, not volcanic gas.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2120. And that kind of deadly gas never occurs naturally...``
Grasping the implications, Miyuki swallowed hard.
At the time, no research institution or public facility that handled such dangerous chemical substances existed.
If such a thing did exist... then...

``...At any rate, it was a close call.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2026. The people I met on-site were planning to use that hidden passage as an escape route... If they had actually gone in, it would have been catastrophic.``
``I'm sorry... I should have warned you more clearly beforehand.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2122. But the fact that you and everyone else came back safe -- that's what matters most.``
``No, please don't worry about it...@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2028. So... what will you do, Minai-san?@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2029. Do you plan to continue this investigation?``

``Well... that's the dilemma.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2124. If we're going to dispatch more personnel for a deeper investigation, I'll need authorization from the Commissioner-General.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2125. What about you, Miyuki-san?``

``I... was entrusted with this by my father.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2031. I intend to conduct on-site investigation whenever the opportunity arises.@k@vS20/40/VTK2_miyuki2032. So, if possible... I'd be honored to join your investigation team, Minai-san.``

``Hehe... you're going to be quite busy with Public Security as it is -- will you really have time for that?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2127. ...But I truly appreciate the sentiment.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2128. When the time comes, I'll reach out to you... so I'm counting on you, all right?``

``...Yes, that's correct.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2130. As I suspected, there appear to be substantial remnants at the site.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2131. As for the gas... yes, exactly.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2132. It still hasn't dissipated naturally.``
``If we can get inside, I'm certain we'll find the canisters used at the time.``

``Yes... I believe there's no alternative.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2135. Seal it off in as natural a manner as possible.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2136. ...Yes, I think the best approach would be to make it appear that unstable ground caused a collapse, burying the underground passage.``
``I'll leave the method to the Self-Defense Force's work team.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2138. Please ensure it can never be excavated again.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2139. ...Very well.``
Setting the receiver down, Tomoe gazed up at the ceiling as if to compose herself... and let out a heavy sigh.
...She was exhausted.
Of all the tasks she had ever handled, this was probably the most unwilling, the most heartrending communication work she had ever performed.
``...@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2142. I'm sorry, Miyuki-san... Akasaka-san.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2143. Ooishi-san too... I'm truly sorry.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2144. And...``

She thought of the two thousand villagers who had been slaughtered without understanding why, without ever even sensing the clear murderous intent behind it... and Tomoe closed her eyes and offered a silent prayer.
Of course, such a self-indulgent gesture could never save their souls.
And surely God would never forgive what Tomoe had just done. Someday, she would be cast into hell.
Even so... there had been no other way.
Those who deserved to be arrested and condemned had already vanished into their graves... there was no one left to save.
Therefore, the Great Hinamizawa Disaster would be buried in darkness as a natural calamity... that was the final decision, reached after consultation with Maeno and the upper echelons.
``...@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2146. We came this far.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2147. We finally reached the truth, and yet...``

Ever since seizing effective control of the Public Relations Office... Tomoe had leveraged her authority and intelligence network to grasp the truth behind the Great Hinamizawa Disaster with painful thoroughness.
The Alphabet Project, conducting research in secret.
The corrupt politician who had used it to line his own pockets.
And a pitiful woman who had been manipulated into committing atrocities, dancing like a puppet on their strings...
The more she investigated, the more she nearly lost herself to rage and hatred.
Just as Miyuki revered Tomoe as a mentor, Detective Ooishi -- who had perished in the disaster -- had been an irreplaceable benefactor to Tomoe.
And his body... had still never been found, even now.
The prevailing theory was that operatives at the time had 'disposed' of him.
And on top of him... that girl, too...
``...Ryuugu, Reina...``
She, too, remained missing.
Though several years later, a hat believed to be hers was found downstream in the river flowing from Hinamizawa, stained with blood... so she was almost certainly no longer alive.
In the end, everything came to a close without anything being resolved -- neither Reina Ryuugu's rampage nor the Nagisa Ozaki case.
And by the time Tomoe had finally risen to a position where she could learn the truth of the disaster... everything was already far too late...
``...I'm sorry, everyone.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2151. I'm so, so sorry...``
Being unable to share the truth with Miyuki, Akasaka, and the others who had poured such passion and conviction into the investigation... was truly agonizing.
But even if she told them the truth now, it wouldn't bring them salvation... it would only deepen their grief.
And so, Tomoe... kept everything sealed within her own heart.
That was what she had decided.
Though she could not shake the doubt that believing this to be the right judgment might simply be her own arrogance...
On impulse, Tomoe opened a desk drawer... and took out a single photograph.
It had faded with decades of age... but the smiling faces of two girls could still be seen there.
``...Thank you, Minai-san.``
She had said that.
The face of a girl bowing with a brilliant smile floated up in her memory... and tears welled in her eyes.
...She couldn't save them.
Not a one.
Not a single soul.
And she herself... had been powerless to do anything.
...Nothing at all!!!
Drops fell upon the desk, again and again.
...God.
Please... I know it's an absurd request...!!
But... just for this one moment, hear my voice.
I don't care if it means... I'm sent to hell.
No matter how painful, how bitter... how sad and wretched the future that awaits me, I will accept it!
So... please.
One more time...
Just one more time... give me one single chance to save those children...!!
...In that moment.
Appearing faintly as if materializing from empty space... a girl in what might have been a shrine maiden's garb suddenly manifested.
Upon her head, something like... horns...
She had never seen her before... and yet, somehow, she 'knew' this girl...?!
``...You are a mysterious being.``
``...Though you know nothing of us at all... though you should be a completely unrelated person, your very existence changes the gears of fate.``
``...Someone who sublimates miracle into inevitability -- a joker in the deck, as it were.``
``The gears... of fate...?@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2156. ...?!``
That's right -- back then... twenty-four years ago, in Hinamizawa.
In a dream... it was her...!!
``...Gears cannot mesh if every one turns in the same direction.``
``...They will not move correctly.``
``...Some among them must turn in the opposite direction.``
``...The gears of fate surrounding us were too strong, and no matter what I did, I could not change their course.``
``...So I gathered a few miracles and guided Rika toward happiness.``
``...Because without that, the flow would never move in the way we envisioned...``
``...But, I realized.``
``...The truth is, there's still one gear that isn't moving. In order to get everything moving, the one gear that fell out of sequence needs to be reset.``

``...It's very small, but if you put it back in place and start to move it, the other gears will move along with it.@k@vS20/12/VTK2_hanyu2051. ...The largest gear, which hadn't budged until now, has started to move in the opposite direction...``
``...There is another valuable, indispensable gear with tremendous power.``
``...And, at first glance, that movement seems to be hindering us.``
``...But with the movement of a single gear, an inevitable miracle will put the dice of fate back in our hands.``
``...That gear is you.``

``...By removing the stagnation from within that fragment that we couldn't remove, you yourself will drift away with that stagnation, but you are one who can transform it into something more tangible.``
``At last, I understand.@k@vS20/28/VTK2_tomoe2162. You're... telling me to become that, aren't you?``
The girl remained silent and didn't respond to Tomoe's question.
But... her eyes were saying it.
``Only you can do this.``
``...Tomoe Minai.``
``...You've suffered greatly in the past, but you're still a person with a strong will to move forward.``
``...We need you.``
``...No, Rika needs you.``
``...We'll be waiting for you.``
``...For the power of your strong will. In Hinamizawa...!!``
``L3 A Fragment of Miotsukushi`` has been changed.
